message:
🐛 Fix race condition in Netlify presence function (#634)

* 🐛 Fix race condition in Netlify presence function with optimistic locking

The user count was stuck at 1 because concurrent heartbeats from
multiple browser tabs caused race conditions in the shared blob store.

Root cause: Netlify Blobs doesn't support atomic operations. When
multiple requests do read-modify-write simultaneously, some writes
get lost (last-write-wins).

Solution: Implement optimistic locking with verify-and-retry:
1. Read current sessions from blob store
2. Add/update the session and prune expired ones
3. Write updated sessions
4. Verify write succeeded by reading back and checking our session
5. If our session is missing (race detected), retry with fresh data
6. Up to 5 retries with random delays to reduce collision

Also adds:
- Strong consistency mode on Netlify Blobs
- Send initial heartbeat immediately (no delay)
- Subsequent heartbeats with 0-3s jitter to spread requests
- Client-side smoothing (max of last 5 counts) for stability

Tested with 10 concurrent heartbeats: 100% success rate.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🌱 Move viewer count from navbar to sidebar

Move the active viewers indicator from the navbar to the sidebar,
next to the Customize button. Makes it smaller and less prominent.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 Fix circular dependency in DashboardPage imports

- Change imports to point directly to DashboardPage.tsx instead of
  through the barrel file to avoid circular chunk dependencies
- Simplify vite manualChunks to put all node_modules in vendor chunk

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
